chunks.text: 1 ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI C.P No.S-564 of 2025 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S). Hearing 1. For orders on office objection No.1 to 6 & reply of counsel thereof as flag “A”. 2. For orders on CMA No.4639/2025 (Stay). 3. For hearing of main case..-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-. Dated 28.07.2025 Mr. Aijaz Muhammad Bangash, Advocate for the petitioner. Mr. Zahoor Shah, Additional Prosecutor General Sindh. Mr. K.A. Vaswani, Assistant Advocate General. SIP Syed Ahsan Ali Shah, on behalf of respondent No.8. PI Amin Alam, on behalf of SSP Malir, Karachi..-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-. Through instant petition, the petitioner has prayed for the following relief:- (a) To direct the officials respondents to provide legal protection to the life, liberty and assets of the petitioner and his families, his family members, friends, employees and well-wishers, further direct them to produce all the FIRs before this honorable Court, in which, the petitioner has falsely been implicated, in Karachi or all over the Sindh. (b) To direct the official respondents that not to lodge any other false and fabricated FIR against the petitioner, his family members, friends, employees and well-wishers and do not arrest the petitioner in other false cases, if any, without earlier permission of this Honourable Court OR Due course of law. (c) To restrain the official respondents and private respondent, their agents, employees, puppets, colleagues, subordinates, workers, person, persons, men, or any other person acting on their behalf from causing harassment, humiliation, pressure, fear and mental torture or threatening to the petitioner, his family members and or book the petitioner, his family members, friends, employees and well-wishers in any of the false and fabricated case in any police station in Karachi OR Sindh, without due course of law.

PI Amin Alam, on behalf of SSP Malir, Karachi..-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-. Through instant petition, the petitioner has prayed for the following relief:- (a) To direct the officials respondents to provide legal protection to the life, liberty and assets of the petitioner and his families, his family members, friends, employees and well-wishers, further direct them to produce all the FIRs before this honorable Court, in which, the petitioner has falsely been implicated, in Karachi or all over the Sindh. (b) To direct the official respondents that not to lodge any other false and fabricated FIR against the petitioner, his family members, friends, employees and well-wishers and do not arrest the petitioner in other false cases, if any, without earlier permission of this Honourable Court OR Due course of law. (c) To restrain the official respondents and private respondent, their agents, employees, puppets, colleagues, subordinates, workers, person, persons, men, or any other person acting on their behalf from causing harassment, humiliation, pressure, fear and mental torture or threatening to the petitioner, his family members and or book the petitioner, his family members, friends, employees and well-wishers in any of the false and fabricated case in any police station in Karachi OR Sindh, without due course of law. From the perusal of memo of petition it appears that the petitioner is involved in FIRs No.49/2025 and 184/2025 registered at Police Station FIA and Gulshan-e-Maymar respectively. Per counsel for the petitioner, in both FIRs the petitioner is on bail. Petitioner has failed to point out as to how he is aggrieved by the registration of FIRs by the district police and FIA police, particularly when he is not resident of the province of Sindh and resides in the province of Punjab. It appears that by way of filing this 2 petition, the petitioner intends to stop the process of investigation which is being undertaken by the FIA and surprisingly FIA is not party to the petition. No case for the issuance of directions in terms of harassment is made out; however, the official respondents are directed to conduct themselves strictly in accordance with law. Needless to say that if the petitioner commits any offence, the police may proceed and record FIR against him, however, he shall be subjected to due course of law. The petition stands disposed of. JUDGE Ayaz Gul
